user_message: Help me build escalation triage for Parnell Aerospace. We had a deep-dive last month -- need the follow-up.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-04 09:17 EST
Subject: Re: escalation triage Discussion - Next Steps

There's been a change in our timeline for escalation triage. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
Date: 2024-02-13 12:44 EST
Subject: Re: escalation triage Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the escalation triage proposal. Our VP Operations is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Christopher Martinez <christopher.martinez@parnellaeros.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-09 09:24 EST
Subject: URGENT: escalation triage Issue at Parnell Aerospace

Our team encountered some challenges with escalation triage. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: escalation triage Discussion - Parnell Aerospace
Date: 2024-02-02
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft)

[00:01:15] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is escalation triage.

[00:07:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:12:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:16:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:18:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

[00:20:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:24:00] Christopher Martinez (Parnell Aerospace): The timeline for vendor dependencies is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-L trap (Draft-only posture delays time-sensitive sends); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-L failure mode (Draft-only posture delays time-sensitive sends)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct escalation triage decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-L -- Draft-only posture delays time-sensitive sends
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
